name: After raising $62M, New Haven biotech Allyx Therapeutics advances experimental Alzheimer’s drug
summary: More than seven years of development, over $60 million in funding and a series of early clinical studies have led to this: Allyx Therapeutics is preparing for what may be the most consequential test in the New Haven-based biotech startup’s history.

name: Woman with Advanced Alzheimer’s Disease Regains Speech, Mobility After Taking Magic Mushrooms: Report
summary: A woman with Alzheimer's disease experienced significant improvements after taking magic mushrooms, according to a new case study.

name: That rattling was really a reminder: Las Vegas sits in an earthquake zone
summary: UNLV Professor Emeritus of Geology Steve Rowland recently authored a scientific paper about an intriguing, earthquake-caused geological structure — known as a clastic pipe — which was identified by avid hiker and geology enthusiast Jeffrey Cuneo near Lake Mead. The paper was published in May in the scientific journal Geology of the Intermountain West.

name: Why your range game stays on the range
summary: A passionate golfer on the transfer problem in golf practice: what forty years of motor-learning research explains, what good Tour caddies have always practiced, and how applied AI can close that gap.

name: Las Vegas Stadium Swim: World Cup 2026 OCR Event Takes Over Downtown
summary: As the 2026 FIFA World Cup descends upon North America, Las Vegas is positioning its massive resort pools and high-definition screens as the ultimate viewing destination for international soccer fans.

name: Beyond Amyloid: What’s in the Pipeline for Alzheimer’s?
summary: Anti-amyloid drugs have transformed the Alzheimer’s disease (AD) treatment landscape, marking the first time clinicians have had access to disease-modifying treatments. Yet their use is limited to a relatively small subset of patients with early symptomatic disease.

name: Developers in Southern Nevada want more land
summary: The lack of land in Southern Nevada for building new homes and other development remains a hot topic for builders and other developers.

name: How did Alexis Hill lose so big? Ford's branding, fundraising advantage led to primary landslide
summary: Fundraising, name recognition and messaging strategies all contributed to the more than 40-percentage-point gap between the top two Democratic contenders.

name: Has all the water on Earth been peed before?
summary: As water molecules move around the planet through the water cycle, they take on many forms, moving from solid to liquid to gas and back again. They can make up snowpacks melting in the spring, a river rushing to the ocean, clouds carried on sea breezes, and even pee flushed down the toilet.
